name: Maple-Sugar Creme Caramel
recipeInstructions:
1. Set out six 6-ounce custard cups or ramekins. Combine 1/2 cup water and 1 cup maple sugar in a small saucepan. Bring to a boil over medium heat. Reduce heat and simmer for 5 minutes, or until the syrup looks dark and shiny and is thick enough to cover the spoon. Remove from the heat and whisk in the butter. Spoon evenly into the bottom of each cup. Allow to cool; the syrup will thicken.
2. Preheat oven to 325°F. Fill a teakettle with about 1 quart water and bring to a boil for the bain-marie (water-bath). Combine the eggs, egg yolks, milk, and maple sugar in a bowl. Mix very well so that the sugar dissolves completely.
3. Spoon about 2/3 cup of the custard into each cup. Place the cups in a baking pan and fill with enough water to come up about halfway on the cups. Cover loosely with aluminum foil and bake for about 35 minutes, or until set. Check to see if the custard is done by inserting a toothpick into the center; it should come out clean.
4. Chill thoroughly. Loosen the custard with a knife and unmold onto a colorful plate. Be sure to serve all the sauce from the bottom of the cup.
